label: Shoulder impingement
notes: Avoid loaded overhead work and ballistic/kipping shoulder loading; prefer neutral-grip, below-shoulder work.

label: Lower back strain
notes: Avoid heavy axial loading, ballistic hinging, and loaded end-range extension (GHD Sit-up); light controlled hinging (e.g., Romanian Deadlift) and spinal flexion (e.g., Sit-up) are acceptable.

label: Knee pain
notes: Avoid loaded end-range knee flexion and impact; prefer box squats to a comfortable height and low-impact cardio.

label: Wrist pain
notes: Avoid loaded wrist extension; use dumbbells/neutral grip where possible.

label: Elbow tendinopathy
notes: Avoid ballistic/kipping pulling; strict controlled pulling and supported rows are acceptable.

label: Ankle sprain
notes: Avoid impact/plyometrics; use low-impact monostructural substitutes.

label: Hip flexor strain
notes: Avoid loaded and explosive hip flexion. The olympic lifts qualify through the pull-under, annotated as hip_flexors: flexion + ballistic — no explicit movement override is needed.

label: Quadriceps strain
notes: Avoid loaded/explosive knee extension; unloaded squatting to a comfortable depth and low-impact cardio are acceptable.

label: Hamstring strain
notes: Avoid loaded hinging, long-length hamstring loading, and sprinting; bike is the preferred conditioning.

label: Calf strain
notes: Avoid jumping, rope work, and running; bike or row for conditioning.

label: Pectoral strain
notes: Avoid loaded pressing through a deep pec stretch; light reduced-range pressing (e.g., knee push-ups) as tolerated.

label: Biceps strain
notes: Avoid high-load and ballistic pulling; supported rows and banded pull-ups as tolerated.

label: Latissimus strain
notes: Avoid the kipping arch-to-hollow reversal and the muscle-up transition, which load the lats forcefully at length; strict supported pulling (Ring Row, Banded Pull-up) and passive hanging are acceptable.

label: Unable to hang from a bar or rings
notes: Limitation, not an injury: the athlete cannot suspend their bodyweight from a bar or rings (hand/grip injury, cast, post-op restriction). Activated by the LLM from the athlete's situation rather than an injury diagnosis.

label: Unable to go upside down
notes: Limitation, not an injury: the athlete cannot adopt inverted positions (vertigo, blood pressure, pregnancy, eye conditions). Blocks supported inversion too, since the head still goes below the hips. Overhead pressing remains available; only the handstand family is blocked. Activated by the LLM from the athlete's situation.
